messages.content:
Traduci tutto in inglese, dandomi eventualmente dei suggerimenti per migliorare la forma:

Lo sport meno prevedibile del mondo

Open on an aerial shot of a large Asian city at night.
Inside a dorm room, a young man ha gli occhi e la bocca spalancati di fronte allo schermo del portatile.
Sentiamo la voce di un telecronista: “Rigore al novantesimo!”
Ma proprio in quel momento, quando la stanchezza sembra avere la meglio su di lui, l’acqua di uno spray gli bagna il volto. È il suo compagno di stanza, sdraiato di fianco a lui: they’re staying up to watch the Champions League, which happens in the middle of the night for them. 
Unopened Heinekens on the table in front of them. 

Cut.
It's evening.
We see a young man in a wheelchair on the top of a long ramp zigzagging up the hillside. È sudato, ma ce l’ha fatta: ha raggiunto il bar.
Appena entra, però, non trova ciò che si aspettava. Tutti quanti sono distratti e chiacchierano tra loro. Alla TV, un messaggio durante una trasmissione dice: “Partita rinviata per pioggia.”
Prima che il ragazzo si faccia prendere dallo sconforto, però, i suoi amici lo raggiungono e gli offrono una birra.

Cut.
Siamo nella mensa di un convento. Una carrellata ci mostra alcune suore sedute una accanto all’altra pregare con le mani raccolte. Ma non stanno pregando per la cena. Stanno pregando per la partita. Il pallone colpisce la traversa e va fuori: le suore esultano con le braccia alzate.

Cut.
We’re in a South American women’s hair salon. Some are under the dryer hood, others are getting their hair dried. 
In mezzo a loro, un parrucchiere ha appena ultimato il taglio a un’altra cliente: il taglio di capelli del suo giocatore di calcio preferito.
La donna è molto soddisfatta, ma proprio in quel momento riceve un messaggio sul suo smartphone. È la foto del suo idolo: ha appena cambiato, per l’ennesima volta, taglio di capelli.

Cut.
We are in a bar. There is a bachelorette party. The bride-to-be, blindfolded, is waiting for a surprise, but her friends are taking their time checking the results of the games on an app: è un pareggio. Refreshano la pagina. 4-3: hanno vinto!

Cut.
Un giovane uomo americano sta modificando una maglietta da arbitro della NFL nella maglietta di una squadra di calcio. Ha anche scritto perfettamente il nome del suo giocatore preferito ed è molto soddisfatto del risultato. In quel momento, con un’espressione rattristita, l’amico gli mostra una breaking news sullo smartphone: il giocatore ha appena cambiato squadra.
I due si scambiano un brindisi con le birre e il giovane uomo comincia a scrivere il nome di un altro giocatore.

Concept: In football it is impossible to make predictions. Even about who is a true hardcore fan.

The Most Unpredictable Sport in the World

Open with an aerial shot of a large Asian city at night.
Inside a dorm room, a young man with wide eyes and mouth stares at the laptop screen.
We hear the voice of a commentator: "A penalty in the 90th minute!"
Just as the exhaustion seems to be getting the best of him, water from a spray mists his face. It's his roommate, lying next to him: they're staying up to watch the Champions League, which airs in the middle of the night for them. Unopened Heinekens sit on the table in front of them.

Cut.
It's evening.
We see a young man in a wheelchair at the top of a long ramp zigzagging up the hillside. He's sweating, but he made it: he's reached the bar.
Upon entering, however, he doesn't find what he expected. Everyone is distracted and chatting with each other. On the TV, a message during a broadcast says, "Game postponed due to rain."
Before the boy becomes disheartened, his friends join him and offer him a beer.

Cut.
We're in a convent dining hall. A pan shows some nuns sitting next to each other with hands clasped in prayer. But they're not praying for dinner. They're praying for the game. The ball hits the crossbar and goes out: the nuns raise their arms in celebration.

Cut.
We're in a South American women's hair salon. Some are under the dryer hood, others are getting their hair dried.
In the midst of them, a hairdresser has just finished cutting another client's hair: the haircut of her favorite soccer player.
The woman is very satisfied, but at that moment she receives a message on her smartphone. It's a photo of her idol: he has just changed his haircut, yet again.

Cut.
We are in a bar. There is a bachelorette party. The bride-to-be, blindfolded, is waiting for a surprise, but her friends are taking their time checking the results of the games on an app: it's a tie. They refresh the page. 4-3: they won!

Cut.
A young American man is customizing an NFL referee shirt into a soccer team shirt. He's also written his favorite player's name perfectly and he's very satisfied with the result. At that moment, with a sad expression, his friend shows him a breaking news report on his smartphone: the player has just changed teams.
The two exchange a toast with their beers and the young man begins to write another player's name.

Concept: In football, predictions are impossible. Even about who is a real hardcore fan. 

Suggestions for improvement:
- It might be helpful to add a bit more context or explanation for each scene to help the reader understand the significance.
- Consider avoiding the use of contractions for a more formal tone.
- It might be helpful to add transitions between each scene to make the piece flow more smoothly.
In football, it’s impossible to predict anything. Even who is an hardcore fan.
Your sentence is grammatically correct, but it might flow better with a slight rephrasing: "When it comes to football, nothing is predictable - not even who is a true hardcore fan."

It’s not unexpected. It’s football.
Everything is unexpected in football. Even it’s hardcore fans.
Your sentences are grammatically correct, but here is a suggestion for a revised version that flows smoother and is more concise: "In football, nothing is certain - not even the most devoted fans."
A volte gli sforzi degli hardcore fan passano inosservati.
Sometimes the efforts of hardcore fans go unnoticed.

Hardcore fans are in the details

We’re near a scientific laboratory in the middle of the Arctic ice. In the midst of a blizzard, a scientist is trying to position a satellite dish.
Appena rientra al caldo e si slaccia la giacca, vediamo la sua sciarpa del Manchester United. I colleghi gli offrono una birra: è riuscito a trovare il segnale della partita per la TV.

Cut.
Siamo in un karaoke in Asia. Stanno cantando tutti insieme Freed from desire quando a un certo punto un uomo asiatico afferra il microfono e comincia a cambiare il testo della canzone in “Pioli is on fire.”
Dal colletto della sua camicia, spunta il tatuaggio dello scudetto del Milan.

Cut.
In the living room of her apartment, surrounded by her child’s toys, a single mother watches the TV with her friend. We see that next to the beer on the table is a switched on baby monitor. Clearly, her kid is upstairs sleeping. 
A un certo punto, the two women cheer excitedly, ma non possono fare rumore per non svegliare il bambino. La donna allora tappa la bocca dell’amica e notiamo immediatamente l’anello con lo stemma di una squadra di calcio.
Le due donne stanno guardando la Champions League.

Cut.
Siamo nella mensa di un convento. Alcune suore stanno pregando con le mani raccolte. Una carrellata ci mostra cosa tengono tra le mani: un rosario, un altro rosario, ancora un rosario… e il braccialetto del Napoli.

Cut.
Un ragazzo americano sta modificando la maglietta di un arbitro della NFL per trasformarla in quella di una squadra di calcio. È un lavoro davvero preciso. Con il ferro da stiro, sta incollando lo stemma. Tira su la maglia e vediamo che sulla schiena ha anche stampato il nome del giocatore.

Cut.
In un bar quasi vuoto, un barista cambia canale passando dalla Champions League alla Formula 1. Ma qualcuno dalla sala si lamenta. Due ragazzi ciechi, con le birre in mano, volevano continuare ad ascoltare il match.
Il barista, sbadatamente, non pensava che potessero esserne interessati.
In quel momento, sul giubbotto di uno dei ragazzi, in mezzo a molte toppe vediamo lo stemma di una squadra di calcio.

Concept: Sometimes the efforts of hardcore fans go unnoticed.

Great job! Your sentences are grammatically correct and express the intended concept well. The only suggestion I have is to make some minor edits to improve the flow of the writing. Here's a revised version that incorporates some changes:

Title: Hardcore Fans: The Devil is in the Details

We're near a scientific laboratory in the middle of the Arctic ice. In the midst of a blizzard, a scientist tries to position a satellite dish. Upon returning to the warmth and unzipping his jacket, we see his Manchester United scarf. His colleagues offer him a beer: he's managed to find the game's signal for the TV.

Cut.
We're in an Asian karaoke bar. Everyone is singing Freed from Desire together when suddenly an Asian man grabs the mic and starts changing the lyrics to "Pioli is on fire." From under his shirt collar, the tattoo of the Milanese crest is visible.

Cut.
In the living room of her apartment, surrounded by her child's toys, a single mother watches TV with her friend. Next to the beer on the table, a baby monitor is turned on. Clearly, her child is sleeping upstairs. At one point, the two women cheer excitedly, but they must remain quiet to avoid waking the child. The woman then covers her friend's mouth, revealing a football team emblem on her ring. The two women are watching the Champions League.

Cut.
We're in a convent dining hall. Some nuns are praying with their hands clasped. A pan shows what they're holding between their hands: a rosary, another rosary, yet another rosary... and a Napoli bracelet.

Cut.
An American guy is modifying an NFL referee shirt to turn it into a soccer team shirt. It's a very precise job. Using an iron, he's attaching the emblem. He pulls up the shirt, and we see that he's also printed the player's name on the back.

Cut.
In an almost empty bar, the bartender changes the channel from the Champions League to Formula 1. But someone in the room complains. Two blind guys, holding beers, were still listening to the match. The bartender, absentmindedly, didn't realize they might be interested. In that moment, the emblem of a football team is visible on one of the guy's jacket, among many patches.

Concept: Hardcore fans' attention to detail often goes unnoticed.
Sono i dettagli a fare gli hardcore fans.
"The devil is in the details" when it comes to hardcore fans.

Traduci in inglese:

Sono i dettagli a fare gli hardcore fans.
It's the details that make the hardcore fans.
È lo sforzo che fa gli hardcore fans.
It's the effort that makes the hardcore fans.

Traduci in inglese:

Fans VS Hardcore Fans

Open on an aerial shot of a large Asian city at night.
Inside a dorm room, two young men are trying desperately to stay awake. As soon as one nods off, the other sprays him with a bottle of water, and vice versa. Unopened Heinekens on the table in front of them. 
They’re staying up to watch the Champions League, which happens in the middle of the night for them. 

In quel momento, ci accorgiamo che il loro compagno di stanza europeo, a differenza loro, si è addormentato con lo smartphone sulla faccia e la maglietta del Chelsea.

Cut.
Siamo allo stadio. La squadra di casa sta perdendo e lo capiamo chiaramente dai tifosi in tribuna, tristi e silenziosi. L’unico a crederci ancora, sotto gli occhi increduli dei vicini di posto, è un uomo disabile che continua a sventolare la sua bandiera pur avendo un solo braccio.

Cut.
Siamo in una città americana. In cosa all’edicola, un turista europeo aspetta di comprare il giornale sportivo quando la sua attenzione viene catturata da un amish che ne ha appena comprato uno prima di salire sulla sua carrozza, parcheggiata tra due pick-up.

Cut.
We are in a bar. There is a bachelorette party. The bride-to-be, blindfolded, is waiting for a surprise, but her friends are taking their time checking the results of the games on an app.
In quel momento, dietro di loro si affaccia un ragazzo con la maglietta di una squadra di calcio chiedendo imbarazzato se può guardare anche lui i risultati: il suo smartphone è scarico.

Cut.
We’re in a narrow street in Naples. Fuori da un bar, alcuni uomini sventolano le bandiere in supporto alla loro squadra.
Ma in quel momento, la loro attenzione viene catturata da due vecchie signore che dalla finestra, come se fossero dei panni stesi, srotolano un grande striscione con scritto “Forza Napoli”.
I tifosi sono stupiti e raise their beers to toast from a distance with the old ladies at the windows, who respond with their own toast. 

Concept: It’s an unexpected extra effort what makes you a hardcore fan.

Title: Fans vs. Hardcore Fans

Open with an aerial shot of a large Asian city at night. Inside a dorm room, two young men are desperately trying to stay awake. As soon as one nods off, the other sprays them with a bottle of water, and vice versa. Unopened Heinekens sit on the table in front of them. They're staying up to watch the Champions League, which airs in the middle of the night for them. 

In that moment, we notice their European roommate, who has fallen asleep with his smartphone on his face and wearing a Chelsea shirt, unlike them.

Cut.
We're at a stadium. The home team is losing, and we can clearly see it from the sad and silent fans in the stands. The only one who still believes, under the incredulous gaze of his neighbors, is a disabled man waving his flag despite having only one arm.

Cut.
We're in an American city. In line at the newsstand, a European tourist waits to buy a sports newspaper when his attention is drawn to an Amish man who has just bought one before climbing onto his carriage parked between two pickup trucks.

Cut.
We're in a bar. There is a bachelorette party. The bride-to-be, blindfolded, is waiting for a surprise, but her friends are taking their time checking the game's results on an app. In that moment, behind them, a guy in a football team shirt approaches, asking embarrassed if he can also check the results: his smartphone is dead.

Cut.
We're on a narrow street in Naples. Outside a bar, some men wave flags in support of their team. In that moment, their attention is drawn to two old ladies hanging out the window, unfurling a large banner that reads "Forza Napoli." The fans are surprised and raise their beers to toast from a distance with the old ladies at the window, who respond with their own toast.

Concept: It's the unexpected extra effort that makes you a hardcore fan.
